original: Pixar's animated film "Inside Out" is released with voices byAmy Poehler, Phyllis Smith, Lewis Black, Bill Hader and Mindy Kaling
George de Mestral, Swiss engineer (invented Velcro), born in Colombier, Switzerland (d. 1990)
Garfield, American fictional cat created by Jim Davis (Garfield comic strip), born in Mamma Leoni's Italian Restaurant
Garfield, created byJim Davis, first appears as a comic strip
Valentina Tereshkova, the first woman in space, returns to Earth
US Viking 1 goes into Martian orbit after 10-month flight from Earth
Search and rescue mission begins for the Titan submarine on an expedition to explore the wreck of the Titanic in the North Atlantic
First full genetic study of cats published in "Nature Ecology and Evolution," reveals they were domesticated 9,000 years ago and are descended from one species, the African wildcat
Heaviest known orange (2.5 kg) exhibited, Nelspruit, South Africa
World's oldest wine, a white, revealed inside a 1st century CE Roman funeral urn mixed with a man's ashes in a mausoleum in Carmona, Spain
US Senators receive a classified briefing on possible UFO sightings by the US Navy
Union GeneralGordon Grangerdeclares slaves free in Texas, now the date the end of slavery is celebrated across the US as Juneteenth

Soviet anthropologist Mikhail Gerasimov opens the tomb of Timurid Empire founderTimurand allegedly finds an inscription stating that whoever opens the tomb shall "unleash an invader more terrible than I." Three days later, Germany invades the Soviet Union.
"Batman Returns", starringMichael Keatonas Batman,Danny DeVitoas the Penguin, andMichelle Pfeifferas Catwoman, is released
Taz, the Tasmanian Devil, a Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Robert McKimson and Warren Foster, debuts in the Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series in "Devil May Hare"
World's then largest sausage completed at 13 1/8 miles long
32 divers finish cycling underwater on a standard tricycle, to complete 116.66 mi in 75 hrs 20 mins
Oksana Chusovitina, Russian gymnast who appeared at a record 8 Olympic Games (Olympic champion 1992; World Champion 1991, 2003), born in Bukhara, Uzbek SSR, Soviet Union[1][2][3]
KSI [Olajide Olatunji] English YouTuber, comedian, and rapper, born in London
Millie Gibson, English actress know for Doctor Who, born in Tameside, Greater Manchester
First plane (F-80) to exceed 600 mph (1,004 kph) - Albert Boyd, Muroc, California
Russian cosmonauts Andriyan Nikolayev and Vitaly Sevastyanov return after nearly18 days in Soyuz 9, the longest crewed spaceflight at the time (eclipsed by Soyuz 11 in 1971)

HMS Kite chases the steamship SS Savannah on its historic voyage across the Atlantic for 3 hours off the coast of Ireland believing it to be on fire. Unable to catch the steamship HMS Kite fires warning shots forcing it to stop and be inspected to the amazement of the British.
First officially recognized baseball game (played by Cartwright Rules) - NY Nine defeats the NY Knickerbockers 23-1 in Hoboken, New Jersey
Red Sox set 3 game record of 16 HRs, all against Yanks
First TV sports and boxing spectacular,Joe LouisKOs Billy Conn in a match broadcast across New York
Dirk Nowitzki, German basketball power forward (NBA Finals MVP 2011; NBA MVP 2007; 14 × NBA All-Star; Dallas Mavericks), born in Würzburg, Germany
Quincy Dushawn Watts, American athlete (Olympic gold 400m, 4x400m relay 1992), born in Detroit, Michigan
Paavo Nurmiruns world record 1500m of 3:52.6; 50 minutes later sets 5,000m WR 14:28.2; also runs WR 3-mile time 14:02.00 within that event in Finnish Olympic trials in Helsinki
KC Royals pitcher Steve Busby throws his 2nd career no-hitter; beats Milwaukee Brewers, 2-0
Evander HolyfieldbeatsLarry Holmesin 12 for heavyweight boxing title
Stanley Cup, Marine Midland Arena, Buffalo, NY: Dallas Stars edge Buffalo Sabres, 2-1 in triple overtime for 4-2 series win;Brett Hullclinches series with infamous "No Goal" ruling
Stanley Cup Final, PNC Arena, Raleigh, NC: Carolina Hurricanes beat Edmonton Oilers, 3-1 for a 4-3 series victory; Hurricane's first Championship
England smash the highest score (481/6) in one day international cricket history in a 242 run defeat of Australia (239) at Trent Bridge
Pete Rose(Cincinnati Reds) andWillie Davis(LA Dodgers) both record 2,000th MLB career hit; Rose, a single in 4-0 win vs SF Giants; Davis, a HR in 3-0 win vs Atlanta Braves
European Space Agency's Ariane carries 2 satellites into orbit
India's APPLE satellite, 1st to be stabilized on 3 axes, launched
Ernst Chain, German-British chemist and bacteriologist (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, 1945 - for his work on penicillin), born in Berlin, German Empire (d. 1979)
Oklahoma writerJoy Harjois named the first Native American US Poet Laureate by the Library of Congress
Rod Coleman, New Zealand Grand Prix motorcyclist and first New Zealander to win the Isle of Man TT (1954), born in Whanganui, New Zealand[1]
Kathleen Turner, American actress (The Accidental Tourist; The Jewel of Nile; Serial Mom), born in Springfield, Missouri

Doug Mientkiewicz, American baseball first baseman (World Series 2004 Boston RS; Gold Glove Award 2001 Minnesota Twins; Olympic gold 2000), born in Toledo, Ohio
Rory Underwood, English rugby union wing (85 Tests England, 6 British & Irish Lions; Leicester Tigers 236 games), born in Middlesbrough, England
Alastair Lynch, Australian rules football full forward-fullback (AFL Premiership 2001, 02, 03 Brisbane Lions; All-Australian Team 1993) and broadcaster (Fox Footy), born in Burnie, Australia
Anthony Parker, American basketball guard (EuroLeague Final 4 MVP 2004, EuroLeague MVP 2005, 06; Maccabi Tel Aviv; Toronto Raptors, Cleveland Cavaliers), born in Naperville, Illinois
Francesco Moser, Italian road cyclist (Giro d'Italia 1984; World C'ship gold Individual pursuit 1976, road race 1977), born in Palù di Giovo, Italy

Ernest Ranglin, Jamaican guitarist (Studio One, Jimmy Cliff), born in Manchester, Jamaica
